places.name: Zugspitze
places.subtitle: & the edge of light
places.storyTitle: As one world sets, the other arises.
story: There is a moment at the peak's edge when the light stops being light and becomes something else entirely — a silence you can almost hold. I stood there until the valley fell into shadow and the mountain stayed lit.

The day-to-night transition here isn't gradual. It arrives like a decision. One frame was enough to understand that some places don't need you to stay long. They need you to arrive at the right second.

places.name: Tangermünde
places.subtitle: & the weight of silence
places.storyTitle: Where the bridge broke, the past arises.
story: Standing at the edge of Tangermünde, where 100,000 ghosts once crossed into the reach of the west. The bridge is gone. The silence carries the weight. The shadows carry the secret.
This is the kind of place where history doesn't announce itself. It waits in the texture of walls, in the way the light falls differently here than anywhere else on the Elbe.

places.name: Schwerin
places.subtitle: & the rules it never followed
places.storyTitle: A fairy tale city that never got the memo to grow up.
story: Castles on lakes. Vintage iron on cobblestones. Cherry blossoms in the frame of an old window. Schwerin plays by its own rules — and the light agrees.
Three posts came from this city. Each time I looked through something to see it: a window, a door, a pane of glass. The frame is never the end. It's always the beginning.

places.name: Stralsund
places.subtitle: & the bird that waited
story: A streetlight, a bird, and five seconds of perfect blue. Stralsund gave me this for free. That's the thing about this city — it doesn't hide its beauty. It just waits for you to stop.
Shot on the Fujifilm X-M5 with the Sigma 56mm. The combination renders blue the way it actually feels: deep, total, unhurried.

places.name: Rügen
places.subtitle: & the impossible blue
story: It just opens — through branches, between trees, across piers where the birds have already claimed the best spots. You find the frame, or you miss it entirely.
The Baltic was the same shade of impossible all day. The sailboats didn't care. Neither did the leaves. Seven frames from the coast. One recurring answer.
